questions.question_text: Let $f(x) = \frac{x}{x-1}$ and $g(x) = \frac{x-2}{x+3}$. What are all the domain restrictions for the quotient function $\left(\frac{f}{g}\right)(x)$?
The graph of a function is given by the equation $2y = f(-3x)$. Which statement correctly summarizes the stretches applied to the graph of $y = f(x)$?

choices.label: A vertical stretch by a factor of $\frac{1}{2}$ and a horizontal stretch by a factor of $\frac{1}{3}$.
A vertical stretch by a factor of $2$ and a horizontal stretch by a factor of $3$.
A vertical stretch by a factor of $\frac{1}{2}$ and a horizontal stretch by a factor of $3$.
A vertical stretch by a factor of $2$ and a horizontal stretch by a factor of $\frac{1}{3}$.
